message: fix(btrfs): reject invalid inode timestamp nanos (bd-h8uh5)

 {"id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","title":"Implement verification runner conventions and machine-parseable result format","description":"## Objective
Implement shared runner conventions for deterministic E2E execution and machine-parseable result publication.

## Scope
1. Standard command envelope and environment capture for E2E invocations.
2. Stable pass/fail JSON result schema with scenario-level details.
3. Exit-code and retry semantics for CI/nightly modes.
4. Conformance checks ensuring per-epic scripts adhere to runner contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runner conventions are adopted by all verification gates.
- Machine-parseable result format is validated in automated tests.
- Inconsistent script behavior is detected and reported with actionable output.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.493066058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:51:44.682751429Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T13:51:44.682038293Z","close_reason":"Implemented verification runner conventions: (1) verification_runner.rs in ffs-harness with parse_e2e_output(), build_manifest_from_parsed(), check_script_conformance(), aggregate_verdict(), merge_scenarios() — 21 unit tests. (2) Extended lib.sh with e2e_emit_json_summary() (auto-emits result.json on exit), e2e_retry() for CI mode. (3) Created run_gate.sh runner wrapper with --ci/--retries/--catalog/--conformance flags, gate-level JSON manifest output. (4) E2E script ffs_verification_runner_e2e.sh with 9 scenarios. Clippy clean, 199 harness tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_verification_contract","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.493066058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.645464252Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.885423555Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.079471552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-h6nz.9.5","title":"Verification Gate: cross-epic verification toolchain contract (unit + e2e + logging audit)","description":"## Objective
Final acceptance gate for the shared verification contract used across all hardening epics.

## Required Validation
1. Unit tests for scenario-ID, log-schema, and artifact-manifest validators.
2. End-to-end dry runs across representative epics proving runner/result-schema compliance.
3. Structured-log audit proving required correlation fields are present and validated.

## Gate Commands
- rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets
- rch exec -- cargo test -p ffs-harness -- --nocapture
- cross-epic E2E dry-run pack with manifest/result-schema validation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Shared verification contract is executable and enforced.
- All epic verification gates are wired to this contract gate.
- Contract drift is automatically detected before release acceptance.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.256817517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T14:18:41.214964113Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T14:18:41.214268861Z","close_reason":"Final acceptance gate for verification contract: (1) Extended verification_runner.rs with validate_scenario_ids(), validate_gate_contract(), structured logging with operation_id/scenario_id/outcome correlation fields — 26 unit tests. (2) Created ffs_verification_gate_e2e.sh with 9 scenarios covering: artifact_manifest tests (36), log_contract tests (17), verification_runner tests (26), scenario catalog validation, cross-epic script conformance, logging field audit, schema version consistency, marker format sync, full harness suite (204 tests). Clippy clean, all 204 ffs-harness tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_verification_contract","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.256817517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.414570202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.570336006Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.724217238Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.886155847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"bd-h8uh5","title":"Reject invalid btrfs inode timestamp nanoseconds","description":"parse_inode_item currently accepts btrfs inode timestamp nanosecond fields >= 1_000_000_000. Those values are impossible for timespec-style nanoseconds and can leak into downstream Duration/SystemTime conversion paths. Reject invalid atime/ctime/mtime/otime nsec values at the parser boundary, update focused tests, and refresh parity/coverage docs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-fs","created_at":"2026-04-27T15:15:25.640891456Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T15:25:02.850372036Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T15:25:02.848281688Z","close_reason":"Rejected invalid btrfs inode timestamp nanoseconds at parse_inode_item boundary, updated adversarial and golden coverage, removed a panic macro in the touched golden test file, and refreshed parity/coverage docs. Validated fmt, diff check, UBS, focused item-payload/inode tests, full ffs-btrfs golden test, package check/clippy, and workspace check; workspace clippy remains blocked by unrelated ffs-fuse test lints.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-h9lpq","title":"[HIGH] 16 KiB btrfs readdir fixture bypasses the shared checksum helper and drifts from parser semantics","description":"# Review finding

The remaining red btrfs directory-walk family is the only synthetic-tree fixture in `ffs-core` still hand-rolling tree-block checksum logic instead of going through the shared test helper.

## Evidence
- Shared helper exists at `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22328-22332`: `stamp_btrfs_test_tree_block_crc32c(image, logical)`.
- That helper is used by the green 4 KiB synthetic-tree fixtures (`crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22357-22561`, `23010-23011`).
- `build_btrfs_readdir_image()` instead inlines its own checksum math at `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23372-23375` while also switching the superblock `nodesize` to `16384`.
- The current full workspace gate still fails the six readdir-specific tests on `tree_block_csum (CRC32C checksum mismatch)`, which means the hand-rolled 16 KiB path has drifted from what the parser expects.

## Why this is high severity
This is now the only remaining workspace-red btrfs fixture family after the default-root smoke fix landed. Keeping a second checksum implementation in test builders is actively hiding the real contract and slowing down recovery.

## Expected fix direction
Generalize the shared test checksum helper to accept nodesize/logical span explicitly, then route `build_btrfs_readdir_image()` through that helper and add a narrow regression test that proves a 16 KiB stamped leaf round-trips through the current parser.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:12:53Z","created_by":"cod-fs","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:36:14Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T20:36:14Z","close_reason":"Already shipped; targeted btrfs readdir repros now pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","fixtures","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}
 {"id":"bd-haq43","title":"EXT4_IOC_CLEAR_ES_CACHE ioctl: drop ext4 extent status cache","description":"EXT4_IOC_CLEAR_ES_CACHE = _IO(0x66, 40) = 0x6628 is the kernel side knob that tells ext4 to drop the in-memory extent status (es) cache for an inode. e2fsprogs and certain debug/ops tools issue it before re-reading extent state from disk to defeat caching artifacts (e.g. after offline metadata edits via debugfs). Pre-fix dispatch_ioctl returns ENOTTY because the FUSE handler has no branch.

Implementation plan:
- ffs-core: add `FsOps::clear_extent_status_cache(cx, scope, ino) -> Result<()>` trait method (default Ok(())).
- OpenFs override: ext4 reads the inode (validates existence) and returns Ok — FrankenFS keeps extent state per-scope (RequestScope-bound), not in a long-lived inode-level cache, so there is nothing to drop. btrfs returns Ok after reading the inode (it has no equivalent es cache; btrfs FIEMAP walks extent_data items inline each time).
- ffs-fuse: const EXT4_IOC_CLEAR_ES_CACHE = 0x6628. dispatch_ioctl branch routes through `ops.clear_extent_status_cache(...)` under RequestOp::IoctlRead scope. No input/output payload (it is _IO with size=0).
- IoctlRecordingFs gains an IoctlCall::ClearEsCache(InodeNumber) trace variant + matching FsOps override.
- Tests: dispatch routes to FsOps with empty reply; ignores any input payload + non-zero out_size per _IO contract.

Acceptance:
- ENOTTY -> Ok(empty reply) round trip via FUSE ioctl path.
- Trace asserts Begin(IoctlRead) + ClearEsCache(ino) + End(IoctlRead).
- Backend errors (eg invalid inode read) surface as ioctl errno not stale 0.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:38:46.846418274Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:02:43.033353170Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:02:43.031062362Z","close_reason":"EXT4_IOC_CLEAR_ES_CACHE dispatch + ext4/btrfs scope-based no-op + 2 dispatch regression tests; 287 ffs-fuse + 708 ffs-core green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-| btrfs item payload decode (ROOT/INODE/DIR/EXTENT_DATA) | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | Implemented in `ffs-btrfs` (`parse_root_item`, `parse_root_ref`, `parse_inode_item`, `parse_dir_items`, `parse_extent_data`) with direct cargo-fuzz coverage in `fuzz_btrfs_tree_items` for deterministic decode and field-level payload invariants. `parse_extent_data()` rejects unsupported extent compression values and nonzero encryption/other-encoding fields instead of treating encoded payloads as raw readable data. |
+| btrfs item payload decode (ROOT/INODE/DIR/EXTENT_DATA) | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | Implemented in `ffs-btrfs` (`parse_root_item`, `parse_root_ref`, `parse_inode_item`, `parse_dir_items`, `parse_extent_data`) with direct cargo-fuzz coverage in `fuzz_btrfs_tree_items` for deterministic decode and field-level payload invariants. `parse_inode_item()` rejects impossible timestamp nanoseconds before they reach `Duration`/`SystemTime` conversion paths, and `parse_extent_data()` rejects unsupported extent compression values and nonzero encryption/other-encoding fields instead of treating encoded payloads as raw readable data. |
 | btrfs open/validate pipeline | `fs/btrfs/disk-io.c` | ✅ | `BtrfsContext` in `ffs-core` |
 | btrfs transaction parity | `fs/btrfs/transaction.c` | ✅ | Transaction begin/commit/abort semantics and integration paths are implemented with coverage in unit + E2E suites. |
 | btrfs delayed refs parity | `fs/btrfs/delayed-ref.c` | ✅ | `DelayedRefQueue` + `BtrfsRef` model + bounded flush/refcount tracking in `ffs-btrfs::BtrfsExtentAllocator`, with queue/refcount/stress tests. The allocator now also exposes fragmentation-aware largest-free-extent discovery from block-group/extent-tree gaps, and `OpenFs::largest_contiguous_free_run` uses that value for btrfs instead of reporting total free bytes as a conservative upper bound. |

+| Btrfs item payload parsers | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover root/root_ref/inode/dir/xattr/extent payload valid boundaries, multi-entry dir/xattr payloads, short headers, length overflows, unsupported extent types, compression values, encoding fields, invalid inode timestamp nanoseconds, zero root bytenr rejection, and malformed ROOT_REF fallback behavior for subvolumes/snapshots |
